docs.title: latamdata-py
docs.text: One-line access to 38+ open research datasets from Latin America.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
latamdata-py is a Python package that provides frictionless access to curated open datasets covering health, neuroscience, demography, and education across Latin America and Ibero-America.

All datasets are:

  • 🔓 Open access — freely available under open licenses (CC-BY, CC0)
  • 📦 Versioned — each release has a persistent DOI
  • 🗺 Georeferenced — data available at country level
  • 📊 Analysis-ready — cleaned, documented, and returned as pandas DataFrames

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text: pip install latamdata-py

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
import latamdata as ld

# List all available datasets
datasets = ld.list_datasets()
print(f"Available: {len(datasets)} datasets")

# Load a dataset
df = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard")
print(df.head())

# Filter by country
df_colombia = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", country="CO")

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to latamdata-py are documented here.

This project follows Semantic Versioning and Keep a Changelog conventions.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• MkDocs documentation site with Material theme
  • codecov.yml for coverage reporting
  • tests/conftest.py with shared pytest fixtures
  • Release drafter workflow for automated changelogs
  • SUPPORT.md with support channels

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Initial release of latamdata-py
  • list_datasets() — returns catalog of all available datasets
  • load(dataset_id) — loads a dataset as a pandas DataFrame
  • info(dataset_id) — returns metadata dict for a dataset
  • Country filtering support via country= parameter
  • 38+ datasets covering neuroscience, mental health, demography, and education
  • GitHub Actions CI pipeline (Python 3.8–3.12 × Linux/Windows/macOS)
  • CodeQL security scanning
  • Dependabot for automated dependency updates
  • CITATION.cff and codemeta.json for academic citation
  • DOI via Zenodo: 10.5281/zenodo.15289327
  • .devcontainer/devcontainer.json for GitHub Codespaces
  • Spanish README (README.es.md)
  • llms.txt for AI/LLM discoverability

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for considering a contribution to latamdata-py! We welcome bug reports, feature requests, documentation improvements, and code contributions.

docs.title: 🐛 Report a Bug
docs.text: Open a bug report

docs.title: 💡 Request a Feature
docs.text: Open a feature request

docs.title: 📖 Improve Documentation
docs.text: Documentation lives in the docs/ directory. Edit any .md file and submit a pull request.

docs.title: 🧑‍💻 Submit Code
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository on GitHub
  2. Clone your fork locally: 
    git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/latamdata-py.git
cd latamdata-py
  3. Set up the development environment: 
    pip install -e ".[dev]"
pre-commit install
  4. Create a branch for your changes: 
    git checkout -b feat/my-new-feature
  5. Make your changes and run tests: 
    pytest tests/ -v
  6. Commit following Conventional Commits: 
    git commit -m "feat: add support for filtering by year range"
  7. Push and open a Pull Request

docs.title: Code Standards
docs.text:
• Style: Black (auto-formatted)
  • Linting: Ruff
  • Type hints: Required for all public functions
  • Docstrings: Google style
  • Tests: Pytest — maintain ≥70% coverage

Run all checks locally: 

black latamdata/ tests/
ruff latamdata/ tests/
mypy latamdata/
pytest --cov=latamdata tests/

docs.title: Adding a New Dataset
docs.text:
To add a new dataset to the catalog:

  1. Add an entry to latamdata/datasets.py
  2. Include metadata: id, title, doi, url, license, description, coverage
  3. Add a row to docs/datasets/catalog.md
  4. Add at least one integration test in tests/test_latamdata.py

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
latamdata-py: One-line access to 38+ open research datasets from Latin America.

Author: Juan Moisés de la Serna Tuya ORCID: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8401-8018 Affiliation: Universidad Internacional de La Rioja (UNIR) License: CC0 1.0 Universal GitHub: https://github.com/juanmoisesd/latamdata-py

docs.title: info(dataset_name)
docs.text: Get metadata about a specific dataset.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: dataset_name : str Name of the dataset (use list_datasets() to see available names).

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: dict Dictionary with dataset metadata.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
import latamdata as ld ld.info("alzheimer-latam")

Source code in latamdata/datasets.py 
def info(dataset_name: str) -> dict:
    """
    Get metadata about a specific dataset.

    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset_name : str
        Name of the dataset (use list_datasets() to see available names).

    Returns
    -------
    dict
        Dictionary with dataset metadata.

    Examples
    --------
    >>> import latamdata as ld
    >>> ld.info("alzheimer-latam")
    """
    if dataset_name not in _REGISTRY:
        available = list(_REGISTRY.keys())
        raise ValueError(f"Dataset '{dataset_name}' not found. Available datasets: {available}")
    return _REGISTRY[dataset_name].copy()

docs.title: list_datasets()
docs.text: List all available datasets in the latamdata-py registry.

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pd.DataFrame DataFrame with columns: name, title, doi, keywords, language.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
import latamdata as ld ld.list_datasets()

Source code in latamdata/datasets.py 
def list_datasets() -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    List all available datasets in the latamdata-py registry.

    Returns
    -------
    pd.DataFrame
        DataFrame with columns: name, title, doi, keywords, language.

    Examples
    --------
    >>> import latamdata as ld
    >>> ld.list_datasets()
    """
    rows = []
    for name, meta in _REGISTRY.items():
        rows.append({
            "name": name,
            "title": meta["title"],
            "doi": meta.get("doi", ""),
            "keywords": ", ".join(meta.get("keywords", [])),
            "language": meta.get("language", "en")
        })
    return pd.DataFrame(rows)

docs.title: load(dataset_name, use_cache=True, **kwargs)
docs.text: Load a dataset by name as a pandas DataFrame.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: dataset_name : str Name of the dataset (use list_datasets() to see available names). use_cache : bool, optional Whether to use a local cache (default True). **kwargs Additional keyword arguments passed to pandas.read_csv().

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: pd.DataFrame The requested dataset as a pandas DataFrame.

docs.title: Raises
docs.text: ValueError If the dataset name is not found in the registry. requests.HTTPError If the dataset URL is not accessible.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
import latamdata as ld df = ld.load("alzheimer-latam") print(df.head())

Source code in latamdata/datasets.py 
def load(
    dataset_name: str,
    use_cache: bool = True,
    **kwargs
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """
    Load a dataset by name as a pandas DataFrame.

    Parameters
    ----------
    dataset_name : str
        Name of the dataset (use list_datasets() to see available names).
    use_cache : bool, optional
        Whether to use a local cache (default True).
    **kwargs
        Additional keyword arguments passed to pandas.read_csv().

    Returns
    -------
    pd.DataFrame
        The requested dataset as a pandas DataFrame.

    Raises
    ------
    ValueError
        If the dataset name is not found in the registry.
    requests.HTTPError
        If the dataset URL is not accessible.

    Examples
    --------
    >>> import latamdata as ld
    >>> df = ld.load("alzheimer-latam")
    >>> print(df.head())
    """
    if dataset_name not in _REGISTRY:
        available = list(_REGISTRY.keys())
        raise ValueError(
            f"Dataset '{dataset_name}' not found.\n"
            f"Use latamdata.list_datasets() to see available datasets.\n"
            f"Available: {available}"
        )

    meta = _REGISTRY[dataset_name]
    url = meta["url"]

    try:
        response = requests.get(url, timeout=30)
        response.raise_for_status()
        from io import StringIO
        df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(response.text), **kwargs)
        return df
    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as e:
        raise requests.HTTPError(
            f"Could not load dataset '{dataset_name}' from {url}.\n"
            f"The dataset file may not exist yet at this URL.\n"
            f"Original error: {e}"
        )
    except Exception as e:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"Unexpected error loading dataset '{dataset_name}': {e}\n"
            f"Please report this issue at: https://github.com/juanmoisesd/latamdata-py/issues"
        )

docs.title: list_datasets
docs.text:
latamdata.list_datasets() -> list[dict]

Returns a list of all available datasets in the catalog.

Returns: List of dicts, each containing: - id (str): Dataset identifier - title (str): Human-readable title - doi (str): Persistent DOI - description (str): Brief description - license (str): License identifier (e.g., "CC-BY-4.0") - coverage (dict): {"countries": [...], "years": [...]}

Example: 

import latamdata as ld

datasets = ld.list_datasets()
for ds in datasets:
    print(ds["id"], "—", ds["title"])

docs.title: load
docs.text:
latamdata.load(
    dataset_id: str,
    country: str | None = None,
    year_start: int | None = None,
    year_end: int | None = None,
) -> pandas.DataFrame

Load a dataset as a pandas DataFrame.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Default Description dataset_id str required Dataset identifier from catalog country str None ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g., "CO") year_start int None Filter rows from this year onwards year_end int None Filter rows up to and including this year

Returns: pandas.DataFrame

Raises: - ValueError: If dataset_id is not found in the catalog - ValueError: If country is not a valid ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code

Example: 

import latamdata as ld

# Load full dataset
df = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard")

# Filter by country
df_co = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", country="CO")

# Filter by year range
df_recent = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", year_start=2010, year_end=2024)

docs.title: info
docs.text:
latamdata.info(dataset_id: str) -> dict

Get metadata for a specific dataset.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description dataset_id str Dataset identifier from catalog

Returns: Dict with keys: id, title, doi, url, description, license, coverage

Example: 

import latamdata as ld

info = ld.info("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard")
print(info["doi"])      # "10.7910/DVN/UVHABW"
print(info["license"])  # "CC-BY-4.0"
print(info["coverage"]) # {"countries": ["AR", ...], "years": [2000, ..., 2050]}

docs.title: How to load a dataset
docs.text:
import latamdata as ld

# Load full dataset
df = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard")

# Get metadata
info = ld.info("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard")
print(info["doi"], info["license"])

# Filter by country (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2)
df_co = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", country="CO")

Suggest a dataset

Missing a dataset you need? Open a feature request and we'll consider adding it to the catalog.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.8 or higher
  • pip (Python package manager)

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text:
The simplest way to install latamdata-py:

pip install latamdata-py

docs.title: Install with optional dependencies
docs.text:
For Jupyter notebook support and data visualization:

pip install "latamdata-py[viz]"

For full development environment:

pip install "latamdata-py[dev]"

docs.title: Install from source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/juanmoisesd/latamdata-py.git
cd latamdata-py
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text:
import latamdata as ld
print(ld.__version__)

docs.title: Upgrading
docs.text: pip install --upgrade latamdata-py

docs.title: SSL Certificate errors
docs.text:
If you encounter SSL errors when downloading datasets, try:

pip install --trusted-host pypi.org --trusted-host files.pythonhosted.org latamdata-py

docs.title: Proxy environments
docs.text:
Set your proxy environment variables before installing:

export HTTPS_PROXY=http://your-proxy:port
pip install latamdata-py

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get up and running with latamdata-py in minutes.

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text: pip install latamdata-py

docs.title: 2. List available datasets
docs.text:
import latamdata as ld

datasets = ld.list_datasets()
print(f"Total datasets: {len(datasets)}")

# Preview the first 5
for ds in datasets[:5]:
    print(f"  - {ds['id']}: {ds['title']}")

docs.title: 3. Get dataset info
docs.text:
info = ld.info("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard")
print(info["title"])
print(info["doi"])
print(info["description"])
print(info["coverage"])  # {'countries': ['AR', 'BO', 'BR', ...], 'years': [2000, ..., 2050]}

docs.title: 4. Load a dataset
docs.text:
df = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard")
print(df.shape)   # (rows, columns)
print(df.dtypes)
print(df.head())

docs.title: 5. Filter by country
docs.text:
# ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes
df_co = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", country="CO")  # Colombia
df_mx = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", country="MX")  # Mexico
df_br = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", country="BR")  # Brazil

docs.title: 6. Filter by year range
docs.text: df_recent = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", year_start=2010, year_end=2024)

docs.title: 7. Save to file
docs.text:
# Save as CSV
df.to_csv("alzheimer_data.csv", index=False)

# Save as Excel
df.to_excel("alzheimer_data.xlsx", index=False)

# Save as Parquet (efficient for large datasets)
df.to_parquet("alzheimer_data.parquet", index=False)

docs.title: 8. Quick visualization
docs.text:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = ld.load("alzheimer-latinamerica-dashboard", country="CO")
df.plot(x="year", y="prevalence_rate", title="Alzheimer Prevalence — Colombia")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("alzheimer_colombia.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
